publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2019-11-18 00:11:03 +0000.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Files for Focus on Art include notes, clippings, publications, and other background materials for the television shows, compiled by Johnnie Parrish. Also, original videotapes of the shows that included interviews with local artists as well as workshops and gallery openings. Also, alphabetical subject files on art and artists. Copy of Seventeen magazine, July 1953, with article mentioning Priscilla Kiefer (Johnnie Parrish)

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Focus on Art was a weekly cable program, hosted by Priscilla A. (Johnnie) Parrish in Ithaca from 1979 to 1987.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Files for Focus on Art include notes, clippings, publications, and other background materials for the television shows, compiled by Johnnie Parrish. Also, original videotapes of the shows that included interviews with local artists as well as workshops and gallery openings. Also, alphabetical subject files on art and artists.
Copy of Seventeen magazine, July 1953, with article mentioning Priscilla Kiefer (Johnnie Parrish)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Johnnie Parrish Papers, #6570. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Focus on Art-The Artist's Psyche, The Creative Process-II
San Francisco Museum of Modern Art 50th Anniversary

Larger reproduction of a photo depicting 3 female students in the tracery of the War Memorial on West Campus
Focus on Art – H. Peter Kahn, Ancient Glass, John Hartell, Printmaking, Jason Seley: Celebration of Sculpture (VHS)
Focus on Art – Jason Seley: Celebration of Sculpture - repeat (VHS)
Focus on Art – H. Peter Kahn (VHS)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: mix up: actually WCNY broadcast of The Three Tenors

did.unittitle: Ameritech, "William Wordsworth and the Age of English Romanticism" (VHS)
2 cassettes, Ed Bryant on Jason Seley
